Prosopis glandulosa, or Honey Mesquite, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Honey Mesquite include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Honey Mesquite include tea, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
